etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 永(永) (yǒng) -> to swim
etymology.interpretation: The character 泳(yǒng) combines the semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") to denote the concept of water, with the phonetic component 永 (yǒng) to indicate the sound. Swimming is intrinsically associated with water, so the semantic aspect appropriately reflects the character's meaning.

examples.chinese: 他每天早上在游泳池里游泳(他每天早上在游泳池裡游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān zǎoshang zài yóuyǒngchí lǐ yóuyǒng.
examples.english: He swims in the swimming pool every morning.

examples.chinese: 夏天到了(夏天到了)，我们经常去海边游泳(我們經常去海邊游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Xiàtiān dào le, wǒmen jīngcháng qù hǎibiān yóuyǒng.
examples.english: Summer has arrived, and we often go to the beach to swim.

examples.chinese: 学会游泳(學會游泳)是很重要的技能(是很重要的技能)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéhuì yóuyǒng shì hěn zhòngyào de jìnéng.
examples.english: Learning to swim is a very important skill.
